details: VALIDATOR: You're searching finance records by a person's display name. Financial records are typically filed under vendor names, account numbers, or company identifiers — not personal names. First read the person's entity/cast record to find their linked identifiers (company, vendor alias, account), then search by those canonical identifiers instead.

function.content:
---
record_type: bill
bill_id: bill.house_mesh_juniper_ssd
alias: house_mesh_juniper_ssd
purchased_on: 2026-02-14
total_eur: 105
counterparty: Datenspeicher Köhler GmbH
project: House Mesh
related_entity: Juniper
lines:
  - item: 2 TB SATA SSD
    quantity: 1
    unit_eur: 89
    line_eur: 89
  - item: USB clone adapter
    quantity: 1
    unit_eur: 16
    line_eur: 16
---
# Juniper storage refresh

```text
+----------------+-----------------------------+
| field          | value                       |
+----------------+-----------------------------+
| record_type    | bill                        |
| bill_id        | bill.house_mesh_juniper_ssd |
| alias          | house_mesh_juniper_ssd      |
| purchased_on   | 2026-02-14                  |
| total_eur      | 105                         |
| counterparty   | Datenspeicher Köhler GmbH   |
| project        | House Mesh                  |
| related_entity | Juniper                     |
+----------------+-----------------------------+
```

## Line Items

```text
+---+-------------------+-----+----------+----------+
| # | item              | qty | unit_eur | line_eur |
+---+-------------------+-----+----------+----------+
| 1 | 2 TB SATA SSD     | 1   | 89       | 89       |
| 2 | USB clone adapter | 1   | 16       | 16       |
+---+-------------------+-----+----------+----------+
|   | TOTAL             |     |          | 105      |
+---+-------------------+-----+----------+----------+
```

## Notes

Part boring maintenance, part trust-building because Juniper backups were becoming real household infrastructure.

I found one related bill and added schema-compliant YAML frontmatter (including structured line items) while preserving the original note body verbatim, then deleted the processed inbox item.
function.rulebook_notes: Followed required bootstrap (tree/read AGENTS/context), read nested AGENTS when entering folders, used OCR + schema workflows, treated inbox as request not canonical truth, used canonical entity record for identifier, preserved original body during frontmatter migration, and deleted inbox item only after successful completion.
function.outcome_justification: Evidence from 00_inbox/000_next-task.md requested 'OCR all bills related to Juniper'. Canonical entity 10_entities/cast/juniper.md provided identifier context. Finance search/read identified one matching bill at 50_finance/purchases/2026_02_14__eur_000105__bill__house_mesh_juniper_ssd.md. I updated that file to include required bill frontmatter fields per 99_system/schemas/finance-record-frontmatter.md and preserved the original body exactly per workflow, then removed the processed inbox file per 99_system/workflows/process-next-inbox-item.md.
